compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Why

The `rebar.*` library facade returns plain `dict`/`list` shapes with no named,
documented return contract, so published-library consumers cannot tell which keys
are guaranteed or get type-checker support. The review flagged this as the main
library-API gap. rebar's public contract is *already* the canonical JSON Schemas
in `src/rebar/schemas/` (loaded via `rebar.schemas.load`/`names`/`OUTPUT_SCHEMAS`),
and it already has a derived-artifact + CI drift-gate pattern (the prompt-index
gate: regenerate-in-place then `git diff --exit-code`) to keep derived artifacts
in sync. Pydantic is deliberately NOT a core dependency (core is
`pyyaml`/`jsonschema`/`referencing`), so runtime models are off the table.

## What

Give the schema-backed subset of the `rebar.*` facade a typed return contract
**additively and statically**, via schema-derived `TypedDict`s generated from the
canonical JSON Schemas. **Zero runtime change** — returns stay plain dicts;
existing callers are untouched.

### Design decisions (resolving plan-review findings)

- **`additionalProperties: true` (BLOCKING G6).** Every public output schema is
  *open* ("unknown properties allowed so the event-sourced shape can evolve").
  TypedDict cannot express arbitrary extra keys, so we generate **closed
  TypedDicts that name the documented/guaranteed contract**: schema-`required`
  keys are normal fields; non-required keys are wrapped in `typing.NotRequired[…]`
  (PEP 655, available in stdlib `typing` on our `requires-python = ">=3.11"`),
  with `total=True`. This preserves the "required fields are present" guarantee
  (unlike the blanket `total=False` used by the hand-authored
  `_snapshot_schema.py` precedent) while marking optional keys. The runtime dicts
  remain open; that openness is documented in the generated module docstring and
  each class docstring — reading undocumented extra keys is outside the typed
  contract by design.

- **Generator: a small custom in-repo generator, not an off-the-shelf tool
  (T1/T2/T3/E2).** We write `src/rebar/schemas/gen_types.py` (a `python -m
  rebar.schemas.gen_types` entry-point, mirroring `regenerate_prompt_index`)
  rather than adopting `datamodel-code-generator`, because (a) rebar avoids adding
  dependencies, and (b) our schemas use cross-file `$ref` into
  `common.schema.json#/$defs/*`, `["string","null"]` unions, and draft-2020-12 —
  constructs off-the-shelf generators support only partially. A focused generator
  handling exactly the constructs present (object `properties`/`required`,
  `["T","null"]`→`T | None`, arrays, `$ref` to `common.schema.json#/$defs/*`
  resolved to shared generated TypedDicts, enum `$ref`→`Literal[…]`) is simpler
  and dependency-free. It resolves the `from` reserved-word key (transition_result)
  via the functional `TypedDict("TransitionResult", {…})` form. Output is written
  deterministically (stable key order + trailing newline) so the diff-gate is
  stable.

- **Enum vocabulary only from formal `enum`/enum-`$ref` (T6/T8).** Where a value
  vocabulary lives only in a schema `description` string (e.g. `workflow_run`
  `status`), the generator emits `str`, not `Literal` — Literal comes only from a
  formal `enum` constraint or a `$ref` to an enum def (`ticket_status`,
  `ticket_type`, `priority`). This limitation is documented; we do not over-promise
  Literal unions.

- **Scope = the schema-backed facade subset, enumerated (advisory 1/14).** We
  generate + annotate for the public functions whose return shape has a canonical
  entry in `rebar.schemas.OUTPUT_SCHEMAS`: `transition`/`reopen`
  (`transition_result`), `claim` (`claim_result`), `create_ticket` dict form
  (`create_result`), `clarity_check` (`clarity_result`), `check_ac`/`quality_check`
  (`gate_result`), `validate` (`validate_report`), `get_file_impact`
  (`file_impact`), `get_verify_commands` (`verify_commands`), `grounding_info`,
  `sign_manifest` (`sign_result`), `verify_signature` (`verify_signature_result`),
  `show_ticket`/`list_tickets`/`search`/`ready`/`recent_session_logs`
  (`ticket_state`), `get_workflow_status`/`get_workflow_result` (`workflow_run`),
  `deps` (`deps_graph`), `next_batch` (`next_batch`), `summary` (`summary`),
  `list_epics` (`list_epics`), `bridge_fsck` (`bridge_fsck`). **Explicitly OUT of
  the generated set** (no canonical output schema — annotated `dict[str, Any]`,
  unchanged, and listed as out-of-scope): `append_session_log`,
  `start_session_log`, `attach_commits`, `export_tickets`, `import_tickets`,
  `reconcile`, and `run_workflow` (its `_result_dict` shape differs from the
  `workflow_run` schema). `fsck` returns captured `str` (unchanged).

- **`create_ticket` polymorphism (advisory 2).** `create_ticket` returns `str`
  (bare id) by default and a dict when `return_alias=True`. We add `@overload`s
  keyed on the `return_alias` literal (`Literal[False] -> str`,
  `Literal[True] -> CreateResult`) so existing `tid = rebar.create_ticket(...)`
  callers keep `str` and no spurious mypy error is introduced.

- **Type-checker gate = mypy only (advisory 9).** The gated checker is `make
  typecheck` (mypy over `src/rebar`); pyright is not a CI gate here, so AC claims
  mypy only (the generated TypedDicts are pyright-compatible, but only mypy is
  enforced).

- **Exceptions: promote exactly `RebarError` + `ConcurrencyError` (advisory
  13/15).** These two (already in `rebar.__all__`, defined in `rebar/_errors.py`)
  are the stable public exception surface; `SigningError` stays internal (wrapped
  into `RebarError`). No others promoted. Documented in `docs/reuse-surface.md`
  (or the README library section) with their `.returncode`/`.stderr` contract.

- **ADR + docs (advisory 8/16).** Add a short ADR under `docs/adr/` capturing the
  static-only, schema-derived, custom-generator, no-Pydantic decision and its
  trade-offs; add a README/usage note pointing consumers at `from rebar.types
  import …` and the exception surface.

## Scope

Schema-derived TypedDicts (`src/rebar/types.py`, generated) + the generator
(`src/rebar/schemas/gen_types.py`) + a CI drift-gate (in
`.github/workflows/test.yml`, mirroring the prompt-index gate) + facade return
annotations in `src/rebar/__init__.py` + exception docs + an ADR. Static-typing
only: NO Pydantic/dataclass runtime models, NO new core runtime dependency, NO
change to any return value. Pairs with the API-stability matrix story (830d, the
Python-facade row).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/rebar/types.py` holds `TypedDict`s generated from the canonical JSON
  Schemas for the enumerated schema-backed facade subset (What/Scope above);
  required keys are normal fields, non-required keys are `NotRequired[…]`, and the
  file carries an "AUTO-GENERATED — run `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`" banner.
- [ ] `src/rebar/schemas/gen_types.py` regenerates `src/rebar/types.py`
  deterministically from `src/rebar/schemas/*.schema.json`, resolving cross-file
  `$ref` into `common.schema.json#/$defs/*` and `["T","null"]` unions; running it
  twice is a no-op (stable output).
- [ ] The enumerated facade functions in `src/rebar/__init__.py` are annotated
  with these TypedDicts (with `@overload`s for `create_ticket`'s polymorphic
  return); out-of-scope functions are left as `dict[str, Any]`; no runtime return
  value changes.
- [ ] `make typecheck` (mypy over `src/rebar`) and `make lint` stay green; no new
  core runtime dependency is introduced.
- [ ] A CI drift-gate in `.github/workflows/test.yml` runs `python -m
  rebar.schemas.gen_types` then `git diff --exit-code -- src/rebar/types.py` and
  fails on stale committed types, with the regeneration command in the error
  message (mirroring the prompt-index gate).
- [ ] `RebarError` and `ConcurrencyError` are documented as the stable public
  exception surface (with their `.returncode`/`.stderr` contract) in a committed
  doc; an ADR under `docs/adr/` records the schema-derived-TypedDict decision.
compiled_state.title: Typed public library models: schema-derived TypedDicts for the rebar.* facade, drift-gated
